‘Permanent Waves’, Rush’s seventh studio album, was originally released in January 1980, and its forward-thinking music signaled a new direction for the Canadian band as it entered a new decade. The six songs encompassing the album encapsulated the breadth of Rush’s formidable progressive chops meshed with its knack for creating radio-friendly arrangements, all elements that were embedded within the grooves of their previous album, 1978’s widely acclaimed Hemispheres. ‘Permanent Waves’ also signified Rush’s first of many recording sessions at Le Studio in Morin-Heights, Quebec, which was at one point nicknamed the trio’s own personal Abbey Road recording studio.

The Exoplanets part 2 | ReYOUniverse","description":"Subscribe to my channel - http://bit.ly/ReYOUniverse

Part 1 - https://youtu.be/djYsSr-d21k

Earth. A planet we know so much and so little about at the same time. This huge 6 septillion kilogram stone became our little home in the vast universe. For us, this place is the most familiar and comfortable. And the fact that it’s just a grain in a massive structure full of mysteries is mindblowing. But is this the best thing that nature can create? Sextillions of extrasolar planets are sextillions of chances for the existence of a new and different world. But what is this world like? Is it similar to ours?... Or perhaps different?

Get ready for an exciting journey to the most breathtaking worlds of our universe. Even some unbelievable worlds…

Just imagine gigantic planets, some of them even more massive than their parent stars! How big are they? Why are they like this? Or terrible exoplanets with extreme conditions where temperatures can rise up to 1500 degrees Celsius; or the very first planet in the universe that is almost 13 billion years old! What could have happened to it during all this time? And of course, some potentially livable planets with much better conditions than here on earth! These planets are made for life! Just imagine what life could be like there! Documentary 2022.

Worlds outside the solar system.

We'll today we'll find out! I'll show you an awesome avocado tree that was grown from a store-bought fruit from the grocery store, and it began producing fruits at 6.5 years old. 
But, was it worth the wait and do the fruits taste good? 
You might have heard about avocados and how they don't grow "true to seed" or "true to type", meaning the resulting fruit can turn out differently to the fruit you start with. Today we're going to talk about if it's worth growing avocados from seed and compare that option to the more common method, grafting - which is used for commercially grown avocados as well as trees you'll find at your local nursery. 
So, we'll harvest some of this trees first ever fruits, and give them a taste to see how they are!
I'll also show you a quick demonstration of how to graft your own avocado tree if that's something you choose to do, and give you 'my take' on growing avocado from seed and if you should give this a go as well.
I hope this video gives some perspective on the topic of seed grown avocados and shows you that this can be a viable option as well... plus it's super rewarding and a lot of fun!

For business inquires and collaborations, send me an email at scottgrowsanavocadotree@gmail.com","channelTitle":"Scott Grows an Avocado Tree","publishedAt":"2023-12-15T20:00:13Z","duration":"PT5M46S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/WJ77cGXteLw/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"p7TuFy_5eek","title":"How to grow an 🥑🪴 AVOCADO PLANT from seed","description":"1- Take out an avocado seed from an avocado, being careful not to damage it.
2- Soak the seed in water for at least an hour, making it easier to peel off the skin (the layer beneath the skin could be light or dark).
3- Use 3 toothpicks and place them around the seed (keep in mind the flat part is the bottom and the pointy part is the top).
4- Fill a jar or glass with water and place the bottom half of the seed in water.
5- Change the water daily with filtered water and leave it in bright light (not direct sunlight).
6- Over the next 4-8 weeks, while the seed cracks open, you will see a root growing from the bottom and a stem growing from the top.
7- When the stem is 6-12 inches, prune it 3-6 inches ABOVE A NODE (this is going to encourage growth).
8- Wait till it’s 6-12 inches again, then transplant it into soil.
9- You’ll need a planter with drainage, a mixture of potting mix, perlite, and peat moss (to enhance drainage and increase acidity), and add a good fertilizer.
10- First, add some soil to the bottom of the planter, carefully take out toothpicks from the seed, gently tuck the roots and plant, and add more soil and press on the soil around the seed to secure the plant (as long as the roots are in the soil, doesn’t matter if seed is sticking out. Lastly, give it some fertilizer and good water.
11- Let your avocado plant dry out between watering; leave it in bright light but not direct sunlight.
12- Enjoy it because you helped create this beautiful plant!","channelTitle":"Tomatoes With Lemon","publishedAt":"2023-09-08T03:49:36Z","duration":"PT53S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/p7TuFy_5eek/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"g0Om8Yx_-XY","title":"Grow your own avocado plant from seed! #plants","description":"","channelTitle":"Brad Canning","publishedAt":"2023-08-23T00:25:32Z","duration":"PT17S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/g0Om8Yx_-XY/hqdefault.jpg"}],"resultType":"MULTI","isMock":false,"page":3,"nextPageToken":"CCQQAA","fromCache":false,"quota":{"used":505,"limit":10000},"timestamp":1750944086532,"lastAccessed":1750944086532},"timestamp":1750964591661,"lastAccessed":1750964591661}

Santana is an American rock band formed in San Francisco in 1966 by Mexican-born guitarist Carlos Santana. The band has undergone various recording and performing line-ups in its history, with Santana being the only consistent member.

LINEUP
Andy Vargas, Michael Tait (vocals); Bill Ortiz (trumpet); Jeff Cressman (trombone); Carlos Santana (guitar); Myron Dove (rhythm guitar); Chester Thompson (keyboards); Benny Reitveld (bass); Dennis Chambers (drums); Raul Rekow (congas); Karl Perazzo (timbales).

ABOUT this concert:
Writing a piece about Santana is not unlike explaining who Santa Claus is: a useless activity because everybody knows the name and what it stands for. Carlos Santana has been in the business for almost 40 years and his music still dazzles many different generations. Whether you got hooked on his soaring, screaming guitar lines during the 1969 Woodstock Festival, or 30 years later when Santana made an incredible comeback with the Supernatural album, the thrill of his music has only augmented because the master guitar player himself has matured to a level that only a few of us  can hope to reach. Supernatural sold over 25 million worldwide. During his North Sea concert Santana, one of the few true legends of rock, will be celebrating a life in music. Don’t miss the party!

Summary: 
Recorded just over a year after Santana played its historic Woodstock Music & Art Fair performance, the band was fast becoming one of the biggest in the world.  They were about to release their second album, Abraxas, and were riding high on the heels of three hit songs from their debut album and a new Top 10 hit, a cover of Fleetwood Mac's "Black Magic Woman." 

A year earlier - in fact only a few days before the Woodstock Festival - Bill Graham staged the first of several concerts billed as "The Fillmore at Tanglewood." Tanglewood is the summer home of the Boston Symphony Orchestra, and the storied venue had just begun to experiment with staging contemporary concerts on their grounds. Staging a rock concert in a classical venue had yet to be done, so of course the concept of doing so excited Graham. He brought the full-scale Fillmore East production team in, including the Joshua Light Show, and booked a great lineup. This first concert featured B.B King, Jefferson Airplane and The Who, and drew the largest crowd that Tanglewood had ever seen by far. 

Buoyed by the event's success, Tanglewood and Graham agreed to stage three similar dates in 1970. The last of these is this show, when Santana headlined after The Voices of East Harlem and the legendary Miles Davis had played (you can hear Mile's set here).

This historic and beautifully recorded performance features a smoking performance by Santana in the last year of their classic lineup, before a young Neal Schon joined the band. We think you'll agree it's an incredible performance by a great band in their prime.","channelTitle":"Santana on MV","publishedAt":"2014-09-26T19:00:16Z","duration":"PT5M42S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/axbtig7w7a8/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"tQfmlE_cvDc","title":"Santana - I'm Feeling You ft. Classic Fender designs, but customised from the factory with the modern player in mind! They feature key upgrades for the modern guitarist and bassist, these super versatile instruments are designed to handle anything you can throw at them!

Check out the whole Fender Player II Modified Range at PMT Online here - https://bit.ly/4lw3mJf

Everything about the necks of these guitars are designed for fast and fluid playability, from the Modern “C”-profile with silky satin urethane finish on the back to the comfy 9.5”-radius slab rosewood or 1-piece maple fingerboard with smooth rolled edges and 22 medium jumbo frets.

The alder body provides classic Fender punch and is available in a variety of exciting new finishes. The Player II Modified humbucker is articulate with plenty of output while the noiseless pickups deliver classic single coil chime and sparkle while eliminating unwanted noise.

For additional tonal flexibility the push/pull switch on the 2 tone control either splits the humbuckers, sets the tele's middle position to series, or adds the neck pickup into positions 1 & 2 on the Strat.

https://www.youtube.com/fender","channelTitle":"Fender","publishedAt":"2024-10-15T15:58:59Z","duration":"PT10M6S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/CxgjINC8U2Y/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"9Bo8pw83Cnk","title":"Fender Starmaster Review | Mooloolaba Music","description":"Prepare to explore a truly distinct corner of the Fender universe! Join Chris, our expert from Mooloolaba Music, as he delves into the intriguing Fender Starmaster. This isn't your average Strat or Tele; it's a unique and often elusive offset design that blends classic Fender elements with an experimental twist.

In this video, Chris will showcase what makes the Starmaster a fascinating guitar for collectors and players alike:

Distinctive Offset Body: A close look at its unconventional, elongated offset shape that sets it apart from all other Fenders, hinting at Jazzmaster and Jaguar influences.
Unique Pickup Configuration: Discover the specific pickup setup (often humbuckers in the bridge and single coils, or other unique combinations depending on the iteration), and how it contributes to its versatile and often surprising sonic palette.
Playability & Feel: Chris will demonstrate its comfortable neck profile and overall ergonomics, assessing how this unique design translates to the playing experience.
The Starmaster's Legacy: A brief nod to its history, often associated with the Squier Vista series or later limited runs, making it a cult favorite.
Tonal Exploration: Hear Chris put the Starmaster through its paces, showcasing the diverse range of tones it can produce, from clean chime to edgy rock.
Beyond the Familiar: Chris will highlight how the Starmaster offers a fresh perspective on Fender design and tone, perfect for those seeking an instrument with a truly individual character.
If you're a fan of unique Fender offsets, experimental designs, or simply curious about a guitar that stands out from the crowd, this deep dive into the Fender Starmaster is a must-watch!

#Rush #TimeStandStill #Remastered #Vevo #Rock #GeddyLee #OfficialMusicVideo","channelTitle":"RushVEVO","publishedAt":"2012-12-21T15:48:43Z","duration":"PT5M11S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/dMSFqXGZ5TQ/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"tMqpkG9HYr0","title":"Ayra Starr - Rush (Live) | Vevo DSCVR Artists to Watch 2023","description":"Ayra Starr - Rush - Live Performance | Vevo DSCVR Artists To Watch 2023

As if manifested by her twinkling surname, Ayra Starr seems destined for the spotlight. Growing up between Lagos and Cotonou, Benin (which are separated by country borders but only 125kms apart), young Ayra feasted on a musical diet of Rihanna, Shakira, gospel, and the first wave of Afrobeats. “I come from a very musical household. My uncle was very musical, my grandad played guitar, my mum used to do reggae when she was younger,” recalls the now 20-year-old, whose creative team mirrors another Artist To Watch alum: Billie Eilish. Like the “bad guy” singer, Starr’s brother is her writing and production partner, and her mother is her biggest champion. Both young women also share a penchant for being unfiltered. “F**k society/F**k you wannabes/F**k your hypocrisy,” Starr jabs on “Ase,” from her debut album ‘19 & Dangerous.’ It was released last August on Mavin Records, which has been home to Rema and Tiwa Savage. As the story goes, in 2019 Mavin head honcho Don Jazzy was in her DM’s an hour after she posted a song tribute to her late father. Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, and NME have also fallen for her intoxicating blend of Afrosoul, R&B and West African pop. High fives were likely passed around when the Nigerian songbird’s fem-empowered “Rush” surpassed 20 million views less than a month after its release this fall; she directed it. That incisive voice of hers was right upfront during her ATW performance of the track, which is yet another addition to her bulging trophy case. What else is there to say: Ayra’s a star.

===========================================================

In today's video, we are going to talk about some interesting facts and myths, about the Tonkinese Cat.

It is often said that the Tonkinese is the only breed to originate from Canada, that is misleading. As a recognised breed of cat, the Tonkinese actually originated in England - where they were known as Chocolates. In 1902 the Siamese Club wrote "One great object of the Siamese Club is to encourage the distinct breeding of the Royal cat of Siam, and the Chocolate cat of Siam, - both beautiful in their own way, but recognised as distinct breeds." The earliest known Chocolate breeders were Mr & Mrs Hurbert Young of Harrogate in Yorkshire. Records show that Young's Chocolate queen, Lady Siam, was successfully shown at the 1888 Crystal Palace Cat Show. The more well-known English breeders of Chocolates, in the late 1800s, were Mrs & Miss Sutherland whose famous Chocolate stud was Prince of Siam.

In the 1960s collaboration, between Canadian and American breeders revived the breed; and in 1967 the newly formed Canadian Cat Association became the first registry to accept the breed, under the name Tonkanese (later changed to Tonkinese).

Here are some interesting facts about the Tonkinese Cats:

1. How Many Kittens Does A Tonkinese Have? 

On 7th August 1970, Tarawood Antigone, a four year old Tonkinese, owned by Valerie Gane of Oxfordshire, gave birth to nineteen kittens. Unfortunately four were stillborn, and fifteen surviving kittens, consisted of 1 female and 14 males.

A normal litter size for Tonkinese is between 4 and 8 kittens.

2. How Long Do Tonkinese Live?

Bill Mainz of Rockville Centre  obtained his male Tonkinese, named Dickens, from the Baikal cattery in Manorville. Dickens was born on 24th December 1991. Sadly Bill had to say farewell to Dickens on 9th December 2013, just two weeks before his 22nd birthday. Brenda and Eddie Cadman also had their Tonkinese cat but their Brown Tonkinese, Hugo passed away in May 2018, less than three months from his 23rd birthday. 

Tonkinese generally live between 13 and 18 years, whereas the average life expectancy of an indoor cat is between 9 to 15 years. Sadly some breeds now have a shorter life expectancy than they used to, possibly due to excessive breeding for type or in-breeding.

3. TONKINESE Cat's Behaviour.

They are normally active and very curious.

They tend to be very vocal. Owners might be concerned for excessive and undesirable crying or meowing, especially at night.

4. TONKINESE Cat's Attention Needs:

Cats in general tend to require a lot of attention. Owners who are home often or are able to participate in activities with this cat breed will be delighted. Try to spend 10-15 minutes actively involved with this breed several times a day. Daily exercise will help maintain its body weight and keep its muscles toned and strong. Toys are important to allow the cat to stay active and engaged.","channelTitle":"Kitten Life","publishedAt":"2020-11-19T10:23:14Z","duration":"PT7M13S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/wtj29QUc44k/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"b2CrHHvfcqA","title":"The Tonkinese Cat 101 : Breed & Personality | Cute & Spunky Cats","description":"Get ready to be entertained and amazed with the cutest and funniest cats on the internet!

#whatif #dinosaurs #permianperiod #prehistoric #earth #planetearth","channelTitle":"What If","publishedAt":"2023-04-26T15:00:11Z","duration":"PT9M28S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/GxyjWhz5VT0/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"np5j_QUv65Y","title":"Prehistoric Planet: What Earth Looked Like 600 to 66 Million Years Ago | Full Documentary","description":"step back in time and witness the unimaginable transformation of planet earth over more than 500 million years. from ancient oceans teeming with bizarre life to the mighty reign of the dinosaurs, this full-length documentary explores how the earth looked, moved, and evolved between 600 and 66 million years ago.

in this cinematic journey through deep time, we uncover how earth’s surface shifted, how supercontinents like panjaea formed and broke apart, and how each era was shaped by cataclysmic events and evolutionary leaps. we follow the rise of the first complex animals, the colonization of land, the evolution of towering forests, the emergence of reptiles and dinosaurs, and the eventual downfall of these magnificent giants.

you’ll explore twelve beautifully detailed chapters, each focused on a major geological period—from the ediacaran beginnings to the dramatic extinction at the end of the cretaceous. guided by nova’s voice, we explore these periods with scientific precision and emotional depth, drawing from fossil evidence, geological records, and the latest paleontological research.

discover how strange soft-bodied creatures lived in the deep ediacaran seas, and how the cambrian explosion brought a sudden burst of biodiversity to the planet. see the great marine life of the ordovician and the devastating ice age that nearly ended it all. travel through the silurian and devonian, when fish developed jaws and legs, and the first forests began to rise. learn how the carboniferous era turned earth into a swampy, oxygen-rich paradise filled with massive insects and amphibians.

the documentary builds toward the shocking end of the mesozoic era—the asteroid impact that ended the reign of the dinosaurs. in a single moment, 75% of life was wiped out. yet from this devastation, new life began to rise. birds emerged from the ashes of their dinosaur ancestors. mammals, once small and hidden, began their journey to dominate the planet. the final section of this documentary explores the legacy of the mesozoic world—the creatures that survived, the ecosystems that rebuilt, and the evolutionary path that eventually led to us.

whether you’re passionate about dinosaurs, fascinated by prehistoric oceans, or curious about how earth looked before humans, this documentary offers a comprehensive, scientifically accurate, and emotionally engaging look at a world long gone—but never forgotten.

every frame, every word, and every timeline is built on real science, presented in a way that both educates and inspires. this isn’t just a look at the ancient earth—it’s a reminder of the planet’s resilience, its capacity for change, and the deep connection we share with the creatures that once ruled it.

subscribe for more long-form documentaries on prehistoric life, mass extinctions, and the untold story of planet earth.
thank you for watching.

Get your Sleepy Lizard avocados at: https://www.guacfarm.com","channelTitle":"SleepyLizard","publishedAt":"2024-02-10T22:16:55Z","duration":"PT1M","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/03spvhzLobY/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"FCIBbSJiDMI","title":"How to Grow an Avocado from Seed","description":"When avocado season swings around I can't resist chucking a few in water to watch them grow. It takes a little patience but it's so fun to see the seed splitting open and that little tap root making an appearance. Even though it's better to move it to soil before the root gets too big they'll grow pretty big in water so if you just want a fun project to watch then keep it in water and if you want to grow it into a pot plant - or even one for the garden - then get it into soil. 

As far as I know, avocados grown from a shop bought avocado seed won't grow any avocados and even if it did, it'd take around 10 years and they wouldn't be the same as the avocado you got the seed from. If you want it to grow fruit you need to wait until it gets big enough and then graft on part of an already fruiting tree.

🌿How to propagate your own avocado tree from cuttings in this easy step-by-step guide! In this video, we’ll walk you through the entire process—from selecting the right cutting to encouraging strong root growth. Whether you're a beginner or a gardener, these simple tips will help you grow a thriving avocado tree at home. With patience and care, you can enjoy fresh avocados right from your backyard. Don’t forget to like, subscribe, and share for more gardening tips!

Avocado trees can be propagated from cuttings, but it can be a bit trickier than some other plants due to their tendency to rot. Here's a step-by-step guide:

1. Choose healthy, mature avocado branch from avocado tree.
The cutting should be about 6-8 inches long and have at least 3-4 leaves.
2. Use aloe vera gel to stimulate root growth. Aloe vera can help prevent fungal and bacterial infections. Aloe vera gel is a natural rooting hormone that promotes root growth. Aloe vera gel helps keep the cutting hydrated, which is crucial for root development.
3. Plant the Cutting
Fill a small pot with a well-draining potting mix.
Insert the cutting into the cocopeat about 2-3 inches.
Firm the cocopeat around the base of the cutting
4. Provide Optimal Conditions
Humidity: Place the pot in a plastic bag or cover it with a clear dome to increase humidity.   
Temperature: Keep the temperature around 70-80°F (21-27°C).
Light: Place the pot in bright, indirect sunlight.   
5. Water Regularly
Keep the cocopeat moist but not soggy.
6. Patience is Key
It may take several weeks or even months for the cutting to root.   
Be patient and avoid disturbing the cutting during this time.
7. Repotting
Once the cutting has developed a strong root system, repot it into a larger pot.   
Use a well-draining potting mix and handle the roots carefully.
Use a sharp, clean tool to make the cuts to prevent disease.
Avoid overwatering, as this can lead to rot.   
Provide adequate humidity, especially during the initial rooting phase.   
Be patient and persistent. It may take multiple attempts to successfully propagate an avocado tree from a cutting.
By following these steps and providing the right conditions, you can increase your chances of successfully propagating an avocado tree from a cutting.

Tonkinese are a domestic cat breed produced by crossbreeding between the Siamese and Burmese. They share many of their parents' distinctively lively, playful personality traits and are similarly distinguished by a pointed coat pattern in a variety of colors. In addition to the modified coat colors of the "mink" pattern, which is a dilution of the point color (as in watercolors), the breed is now being shown in the foundation-like Siamese and Burmese colors: pointed with white and Solid overall (sepia.) They are also now designated a natural breed, as their history has now determined them to have been extant since the 14th Century.

More specifically, I was focused on a father I saw in a news interview on the evening of the Parkland shooting who was expressing his frustration over the fact that he could not remember if he had said goodbye to his daughter as she left their home for school on the day she was killed. His words were devastating to me as I could not imagine what it must be like to lose a child in such a horrific manner. While knowing that everyone will interpret the message behind ‘All I Need Is You’ in their own way, it is my hope that this song can provide comfort to someone who is processing some sort of loss in their life." -Brandon Jenner

{"data":{"success":true,"videos":[{"id":"pnANE6PfH90","title":"How to grow avocados with an avocado from the supermarket! creative explained","description":"","channelTitle":"creative explained","publishedAt":"2025-03-25T16:32:56Z","duration":"PT1M46S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/pnANE6PfH90/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"LKQ7k--xhHA","title":"DIY Avocado Tree with an Avocado from the Store | creative explained","description":"","channelTitle":"creative explained","publishedAt":"2023-04-20T16:27:00Z","duration":"PT58S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/LKQ7k--xhHA/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"lIzofXhaVrg","title":"Grow an avocado tree in your backyard! creative explained","description":"","channelTitle":"creative explained","publishedAt":"2025-05-29T14:14:36Z","duration":"PT1M31S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/lIzofXhaVrg/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"uwzbt3Ie7II","title":"5 Best Avocado Trees to Grow | NatuerHills.com","description":"Hey guys, it’s Whit from Nature Hills, who doesn’t like avocados? From making tangy guacamole to smooth avocado toast, avocados make a quick and nutritious snack - but they can be tough on the budget. Which makes the fact that you can grow them right in your backyard extremely appealing for most. The question isn’t, should I grow an avocado tree, it’s WHICH avocado tree should I grow! 

Before we get into it - it’s worth mentioning that many avocado trees make lovely indoor potted plants for those of us who aren’t lucky enough to live in the tropical climates of zones 9-11. Follow the same general rules for growing citrus indoors and you’ll find yourself with a happy avocado tree year-round.

We have an incredible selection of avocado trees and I want to tell you all about our favorite 5, so let's get started here!

#5 Monroe Avocado Tree
This glossy, dark-green avocado stands out with its light-green flowers that appear with star-shaped blossoms throughout the spring. With its unique blossoms, it’s sure to catch the eye, but we know that’s not why you’re growing it! The avocados tend to ripen later in the season. This tree does great planted outside in full sun or used as a large houseplant if you have a nearby sunny window. #5 Monroe Avocado Tree - Growing zones 9-11 (Indoors 4-11), dense lush evergreen foliage, and are great indoors.
Shop here: https://www.naturehills.com/monroe-avocado-tree 

#4 Hall Avocado Tree
You know that classic avocado shape? The one that kind of looks like a pear? The Hall Avocado does that! This special avocado has bright green skin and has a light-green creamy color on the inside once ripe! Use it in your guacamole or various other summer salads. Hall will grow to be a pretty formidable tree outdoors but can be kept compact with pruning to fit right into your kitchens during the winter. #4 Hall Avocado Tree - Growing zones 9-11 (Indoors 4-10), sun-loving fantastic broad-leaf evergreen and makes a great privacy tree for those of you in the south.
Shop here: https://www.naturehills.com/hall-avocado-tree

#3 Donnie Avocado Tree
Pollinators will flock to your yard for a chance to snack on the nectar from these trees. Donnie is a really interesting variety because you’ll want to plant this type ‘A’ avocado with a type ‘B’ avocado so that you can have a larger crop of fruit. A type ‘A’ avocado is when the flowers open female in the morning , and change to a male flower that afternoon. Other than that, Donnie is a low-maintenance tree that loves full-sun. #3 Donnie Avocado Tree - Growing zones 9-11 (Indoors 4-11), attracts pollinators, evergreen foliage and sun-loving.
Shop here: https://www.naturehills.com/donnie-avocado-tree

#2 Bacon Avocado Tree
I mean, this avocado has just - the best- name. With its smooth green and easy to peel skin you’ll love these avocados for smoothies, salads, garnishes, and more. Plant this as a specimen tree in your front or back yard for a great harvest! Just wait until you see its glossy dark green leaves that give your yard a tropical forest vibe. These are self-fertile but like other fruit trees, having both a type A and a type B plant nearby will produce larger crops.  #2 Bacon Avocado Tree - Growing zones 9-11 (4-11 indoors), late season harvest and self-fertile.
Shop here: https://www.naturehills.com/avacodo-tree-bacon

#1 Hass Avocado Tree
Last but not least, we picked Hass Avocado to be our #1 choice. It’s hard not to when something like 80% of avocados grown worldwide are Hass! With its beautiful green-skinned, oval fruit and glossy foliage it’s sure to look picture-perfect planted in your yard or in a pot in a sunny window indoors. Hass has a durable fruit that is prized for it’s shelf-life and flavor. When planting, remember to mulch your trees in about 4 inches of mulch and to keep an eye out for overwatering. #1 Hass Avocado Tree- Growing zones outdoor 9-11, or on the patio and indoors 4-11,  full sun, and the world's most popular avocado tree.
Shop here: https://www.naturehills.com/hass-avocado-trees

Did you know the flowers on an avocado are unique because they are neither male or female, but open as one sex in the morning, and revert to the other sex by the next day in the afternoon. If that feels confusing, don’t worry, all you need to know is whether you have a type A or type B and simply pair accordingly. All that information can be found on our product pages or on our #ProPlantTips Blog.

As always, if you like our content, please consider leaving a like and subscribing to our channel. For more plant info and inspiration, check out our Facebook, Instagram, Pinterest, and Twitter.

Until next time, Happy Planting!","channelTitle":"Nature Hills Nursery","publishedAt":"2023-05-05T17:50:27Z","duration":"PT4M7S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/uwzbt3Ie7II/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"LYY_F4QEIUE","title":"🌿Grow Avocado Trees Faster with These Simple Tips!","description":"👉Join this channel to get access to perks:

The best time to propagating Avocado Tree by air layering methods is during the spring or early summer months. This is because the weather is warm and humid, which encourages root growth.

⭐⭐Specifically, the best months to propagating Avocado Tree by air layering method are:
Spring: March, April, May
Summer: June, July, August

The Go-To Avocado Tree for Cold Climates
Why Cold Hardy Avocado Trees?

Home-grown avocados, no matter where you live. With the Cold Hardy Avocado Tree, it's possible. This strong tree withstands frigid temperatures as low as 20 degrees, living up to its name.

Simply plant your Avocado Tree in a container and bring it inside during the winter months - it will continue to grow. You’ll get an abundant yield of fruit, year after year, and even faster than you would with seed-grown varieties. Basically, the Cold Hardy Avocado's fast growth means you're going to enjoy your avocados in at least three years instead of the 10 years or more it takes to grow fruit from seedlings.

Common Questions About Cold Hardy Avocado Trees

#crazyfacts","channelTitle":"Mahian Culture","publishedAt":"2024-10-25T10:30:21Z","duration":"PT16S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/jSyeLV-ZHN8/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"o4P12sGQoyk","title":"The 23 Million Year Old Human-like Skull","description":"For more information about STEM School Highlands Ranch, just visit our website:            https://stemk12.org 

The evolution of humans, scientifically known as Homo sapiens, is a remarkable journey that spans millions of years. It is a story of adaptation, innovation, and survival that has led to the emergence of modern humans as the dominant species on Earth. Here's an overview of the key milestones in the evolution of humans:

Primate Ancestors (Approximately 60-70 Million Years Ago): The story of human evolution begins with our shared ancestry with other primates, including chimpanzees, gorillas, and bonobos. Our common ancestors were small, tree-dwelling mammals that lived in Africa.

Australopithecus (Approximately 4-2 Million Years Ago): The earliest members of our human lineage, known as australopithecines, were bipedal (walked on two legs) and lived in Africa. The most famous of these is Australopithecus afarensis, represented by the fossil "Lucy." Bipedalism was a crucial adaptation that allowed them to explore new habitats and access food resources.

Homo habilis (Approximately 2.4-1.4 Million Years Ago): Homo habilis, or "handy man," was one of the first species to use tools. This marked a significant advancement in human evolution, as tools allowed for better processing of food and the development of more complex behaviors.

Homo erectus (Approximately 1.9 Million to 140,000 Years Ago): Homo erectus had a more advanced toolkit and was the first hominin species to leave Africa. They spread to various parts of Asia and Europe and are believed to have controlled fire, which provided warmth, protection, and a means to cook food.

Homo neanderthalensis (Neanderthals) (Approximately 400,000 to 40,000 Years Ago): Neanderthals, a distinct human species, lived in Europe and western Asia. They were skilled hunters and toolmakers, demonstrating complex behaviors and symbolic thinking. Genetic evidence suggests that some interbreeding occurred between Neanderthals and Homo sapiens.

Homo sapiens (Modern Humans) (Approximately 300,000 to Present): Homo sapiens, our species, originated in Africa and gradually developed advanced cognitive abilities, complex language, and cultural innovations. Around 70,000 to 100,000 years ago, early modern humans began migrating out of Africa, eventually populating the entire world. This migration is thought to have been driven by factors such as climate change and the search for new resources.

Agricultural Revolution (Approximately 10,000 Years Ago): The transition from hunting and gathering to agriculture marked a profound shift in human society. It led to the development of settled communities, the rise of civilizations, and the growth of human populations.

Industrial Revolution (Late 18th Century): The Industrial Revolution marked another pivotal point in human history, with rapid advancements in technology, transportation, and industry. This era brought about significant changes in the way people lived and worked, leading to urbanization and the modernization of societies.

Contemporary Developments: The 20th and 21st centuries have seen unprecedented scientific, technological, and cultural progress. Humans have landed on the moon, developed sophisticated communication systems, and made substantial medical and scientific breakthroughs.

Throughout this evolutionary journey, humans have demonstrated an extraordinary capacity for adaptation, innovation, and cooperation. Our development of complex societies, languages, and technologies has set us apart from all other species on Earth. The story of human evolution is an ongoing narrative, shaped by our continued exploration, discovery, and ability to shape our own destiny on this planet.","channelTitle":"STEM News Network ","publishedAt":"2023-09-18T19:19:13Z","duration":"PT44S","thumbnail":"https://i.ytimg.com/vi/o4P12sGQoyk/hqdefault.jpg"},{"id":"WMCD7QDVc4Q","title":"THIS WAS THE FIRST PLANET IN OUR SOLAR SYSTEM 🌍🪐😱 #shorts","description":"Have you ever wondered about the first planet that was created in our solar system? In this 20-second YouTube short video, we explore the fascinating story of "Theia", a small planet that was formed before Earth. Scientists believe that Theia collided with Earth, and the debris from the collision formed the moon. Join us as we take a quick look at the early solar system and discover the incredible origin of our closest celestial neighbor.
